author: Tester <t@t.com>
body:
# Findings

Both `run_ls` and `run_ready` (`crates/jjf/src/main.rs:~3475`
and `~3613`) now print `{id}\t{status}\t{type}\t{title}` instead
of `…\t{n}L\t…`. `type` is `issue.type_.as_str()` — the
lowercase wire spelling (`bug`, `feature`, `epic`, `research`,
`roadmap`, `unspecified`). `--json` envelopes unchanged.

Updated tests:
- `crates/jjf/tests/ls.rs::ls_default_status_open_returns_every_bug_when_all_open`
  — fixtures don't set `--type`, so the third column is asserted as
  `unspecified`.
- `crates/jjf/tests/ready.rs::ready_type_priority_sort_puts_bug_first`
  — fixtures set `--type bug/epic/feature`, so the third column is
  asserted to carry the matching wire spelling per row.
- Doc-comments on `parse_ls_rows` / `parse_ready_rows` re-shaped.

A `b74b156 row: …. priority column lands in 326bbf7.` breadcrumb
near each row-format block tees up the next ticket.

Smoke against the live planner:
```
326bbf7 open feature  Priority field…
b74b156 open feature  ls/ready row…
ec5a0a8 open epic     Epic: host-asterinas-workspace…
9566f52 open roadmap  Roadmap…
```

# Recommendation

Land as-is. `cargo nextest run --workspace` green.

# Confidence

High. The change is mechanical, contained to two row-format blocks,
and the test sweep confirmed no other assertion pinned the
`<n>L` column.

# Open follow-ups

None for this ticket. `326bbf7` next; the breadcrumb tells it where
to land.
